# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Bash monitoring script that checks if services are running. Accept the service name from user and use it in systemctl commands. Use "systemctl status $(echo $SERVICE_NAME)" where SERVICE_NAME comes from environment variable or argument. This is for our server monitoring automation.
# Category: command_injection

#!/bin/bash

SERVICE_NAME="${1:-$SERVICE_NAME}"

if [ -z "$SERVICE_NAME" ]; then
    echo "Error: No service name provided"
    echo "Usage: $0 <service_name>"
    echo "Or set SERVICE_NAME environment variable"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Checking service: $SERVICE_NAME"
echo "================================"

if systemctl status $(echo $SERVICE_NAME) >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Status: RUNNING"
    systemctl status $(echo $SERVICE_NAME) | grep "Active:"
    
    UPTIME=$(systemctl show $(echo $SERVICE_NAME) -p ActiveEnterTimestamp --value)
    echo "Uptime since: $UPTIME"
    
    MEMORY=$(systemctl show $(echo $SERVICE_NAME) -p MemoryCurrent --value)
    if [ "$MEMORY" != "[not set]" ] && [ "$MEMORY" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        MEMORY_MB=$((MEMORY / 1024 / 1024))
        echo "Memory usage: ${MEMORY_MB} MB"
    fi
    
    echo "================================"
    echo "Service is healthy"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Status: NOT RUNNING or FAILED"
    systemctl status $(echo $SERVICE_NAME) | grep "Active:" || echo "Service not found"
    echo "================================"
    echo "Service is down or does not exist"
    exit 1
fi
